mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in PAYEMS signals expanding household income and lower near-term recession risk, causing equity investors to raise expected corporate revenues and bid the S&P 500 higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value before 2014-12-15 was 140045 thousand
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with positive payroll momentum
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month PAYEMS growth does not produce positive 20-day S&P 500 return spreads versus lower PAYEMS growth in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, the policy-rate anchor suppresses cash yields and discount rates, increasing the relative attractiveness of equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value before 2014-12-15 was 0.09
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS < 0.25% observations have lower mean 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations, or if the triggered hit rate is below 50% over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in UNRATE below 6.0% after a high-unemployment recovery signals improving labor utilization and consumer demand, leading analysts to revise earnings expectations upward and producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value before 2014-12-15 was 5.8
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 20-day forward returns following UNRATE crossings below 6.0% are not higher than unconditional 20-day returns, or if positive-return frequency is below 50% in triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS rises faster than CPIAUCSL, real retail spending is expanding, which raises expected near-term corporate revenues and margins for consumer-exposed firms and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value before 2014-12-15 was 449282 million
fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value before 2014-12-15 was 237.642
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term of RSAFS growth minus CPIAUCSL growth has non-positive predictive slope for 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward regressions, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp 3-month contraction in INDPRO signals weakening production, inventory adjustment, and declining operating leverage, causing investors to lower earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value before 2014-12-15 was 106.6617
regime_hint: growth slowdown or industrial recession scare
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contractions are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if triggered mean returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A fall in HOUST below 1000 thousand annualized units signals weakening rate-sensitive household demand and construction activity, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value before 2014-12-15 was 1009 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST crossings below 1000 are followed by 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional mean in walk-forward evaluation, or if the negative signal has a hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero while UNRATE is falling, monetary policy remains accommodative despite improving labor conditions, supporting risk appetite and earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value before 2014-12-15 was 0.09
fact:UNRATE latest available value before 2014-12-15 was 5.8
regime_hint: zero lower bound expansion with falling unemployment
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and falling UNRATE does not outperform periods with only one condition satisfied, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while UNRATE is below 6.0%, investors anticipate earlier policy tightening and higher discount rates, causing valuation compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value before 2014-12-15 was 237.642
fact:UNRATE latest available value before 2014-12-15 was 5.8
regime_hint: late-cycle normalization risk under low policy rates
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-acceleration and low-unemployment observations are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if returns are not lower than baseline event-window returns.
